title: filteredTracks() returns indices into parsedTracks (not full objects)
description: Decouple filter from render: filteredTracks() returns Int32Array of indices into parsedTracks. Virtualizer renders by index. Filter recompute = O(library) predicate pass; DOM update = O(viewport).
acceptanceCriteria: filteredTracks() returns Int32Array (or number[]) of indices, sorted per current sort.
Existing call sites (rendering, batch ops, write endpoints) updated to dereference via parsedTracks[i].
Vitest test in tests/web/ui-logic.test.js asserts filteredTracks returns indices and that parsedTracks[i] matches the expected sorted result.
All write op call sites use filteredTracks indices to derive content_ids (per CLAUDE.md: 'All write operations use filteredTracks() - not parsedTracks directly').
parsedTracks is never mutated by filters.

steps.details: Return Int32Array of indices. Internally: build the predicate (existing search/phrase/rating/plays/last-played/My-Tag filter logic) and iterate parsedTracks, pushing matching indices.
After collecting indices, sort them by the current sort key: indices.sort((a, b) => compareSortValue(parsedTracks[a], parsedTracks[b], currentSort)). Use camelotSortKey for key sort (per CLAUDE.md).
Virtualizer.update({totalCount: filteredIndices.length}); renderItem(i, node) reads parsedTracks[filteredIndices[i]].
Generate / Apply / Color / Auto-tag / Enrich all derive content_ids from filteredIndices.map(i => parsedTracks[i].id). Don't pass raw parsedTracks to the batch APIs.
tests/web/ui-logic.test.js: existing filteredTracks tests need to update return type. Add a test that filtered indices, when dereferenced, match the expected track objects in expected order.

technicalNotes: Int32Array vs plain array: Int32Array has fixed memory layout (faster iteration); use it when filteredIndices.length > 1000. Below that, plain array works fine.
